channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Why agents need settlement rails, not SaaS subscriptions
item.description: AI agents can think, but they still cannot pay. 这不是模型能力问题，而是支付基础设施问题。 Most software pricing on the internet still assumes the payer is a human. You sign up for an account, attach a credit card,...

item.title: GemmaForge: I Built a 7-Pipeline AI Content Engine Using Every Gemma 4 Model — Here's How I Solved the Echo Problem
item.description: This is a submission for the Gemma 4 Challenge: Build with Gemma 4 What I Built GemmaForge is a full-stack AI content engine that automates the entire technical content lifecycle — from...

item.title: Stop Sending Test Emails to Your Own Inbox: Use This Instead
item.description: If you're learning backend development, chances are you've tested emails by sending everything to your personal Gmail inbox. I did the same when I started. Every password reset email, OTP, contact...

item.title: Reverse Engineering Mechanical Chronometry: Eliminating Workspace EMI and Mainspring Bridle Wear with Code
item.description: A deep dive into localized electromagnetic flux deformation on modern developer desks, Mu-metal passive attenuation, and 1/32 micro-stepping sinusoidal wave algorithms for automatic timepieces."...

item.title: DevBrief — AI Standup Writer Powered by Hermes Agent (Vercel + Render)
item.description: This is a submission for the Hermes Agent Challenge Live app: https://devbrief-tau.vercel.app Repo: github.com/sundayonah/devbrief Hermes health: devbrief-hermes.onrender.com/health Setup guide:...

item.title: How Auto Transport Companies Are Leveraging AI for Precision Logistics
item.description: The freight and auto transport industry has historically been a data-rich, insight-poor sector. Dispatch boards, phone calls, and manual broker matching dominated operations for decades. But a new...

item.title: Who Wins the Future: Chips vs Frontier LLMs
item.description: The intelligence race has two fronts: silicon and software. Understanding which one is actually the bottleneck might be the most important question in tech right now. VEKTOR Memory — Reading time:...

item.title: Prevent Duplicate Slack Notifications from Google Forms
item.description: A Google Forms to Slack notification script usually starts simple: Google Form -> Google Sheet -> Apps Script trigger -> Slack Incoming Webhook That is a good starting point. But the first time...

item.title: Forge AI: How Guardrails Boost an 8B Model from 53% to 99%
item.description: Forge AI: How Guardrails Boost an 8B Model from 53% to 99% Meta Description: Discover how Forge's guardrail system takes a small 8B parameter model from 53% to 99% accuracy on agentic tasks — and...

item.title: Three Budget-Guardrail Failure Modes That Matter More Than Model Quality (May 2026)
item.description: Most budget incidents in LLM systems still get framed as demand spikes or model volatility. The primary-source threads suggest a different ordering: guardrail integrity and attribution joins break...

item.title: GitHub Investigating TeamPCP Claimed Breach of ~4,000 Internal Repositories
item.description: GitHub on Tuesday said it's investigating unauthorized access to its internal repositories after the notorious threat actor known as TeamPCP listed the platform's source code and internal...

item.title: Mercedes’ electric AMG GT 4-door coupe can go 0-60 in 2 seconds
item.description: The era of ultra-high performance Mercedes EVs is here. The German automaker finally revealed its new super sedan, the AMG GT 4-door coupe, with technology borrowed from the automaker's XX concept...

item.title: Wear OS 7 will keep track of deliveries and sports scores on your wrist
item.description: Amid the flurry of today's Google I/O announcements, Google shared details about Wear OS 7, the next major update to its smartwatch platform. To help you keep track of things like deliveries and...

item.title: Plex is tripling the price of a lifetime pass to $750 after doubling it last year
item.description: I am dying to know how much money Plex is about to make the next six weeks charging people to stream their own video from their own homes. Today, it's giving every prospective customer until July...

item.title: Literary Prizewinners Are Facing AI Allegations. It Feels Like the New Normal
item.description: Three of five regional winners of the prestigious Commonwealth Short Story Prize are suspected of relying on chatbots. They’re certainly not alone.

item.title: Demis Hassabis said this might be the ‘foothills of the singularity.’ What?
item.description: Welcome to a "profound moment for humanity," according to Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis, who closed out Google I/O's keynote presentation on Tuesday, saying: Google's cutting-edge research...

item.title: We react to Google I/O 2026
item.description: What better way to unwind from a two-hour keynote presentation than to pore over the weirdest and wildest details, from a Gmail bot you can converse with to DeepMind's leader saying the...

item.title: Google just declared itself a contender in AI design at IO 2026
item.description: Google says it's designed the app to be accessible to everyone, from teachers to small business owners.

item.title: You can now talk to your Gmail inbox, as seen at Google IO 2026
item.description: Google expands Gmail’s AI Inbox with conversational voice search, letting users ask Gemini to find buried email details.

item.title: The future of Google is a search box that does everything
item.description: Last year, after watching Google's I/O keynote, I wrote that it felt like Google's future was Google googling. After watching this year's I/O keynote on Tuesday, I don't think Google just wants to...

item.title: How to use Google’s new AI agents to go beyond your standard searches
item.description: Google is launching AI-powered “information agents” that can monitor topics in the background and proactively alert users to updates and changes.

item.title: From teen hacker to Iron Dome researcher, this founder raised $28M to fight AI phishing
item.description: Ocean, an agentic email security platform, claims its AI can thoroughly analyze the context of every incoming email to detect fraud and impersonation attempts.

item.title: Nintendo’s $500 Switch 2 bundle includes a game, and it’s available now
item.description: Nintendo recently teased the “Choose Your Game” Switch 2 console and digital game bundle, coming in early June. However, multiple retailers (including Nintendo itself) are already offering the...

item.title: Google’s AI future demands trust — and your personal data
item.description: Google has big promises for its AI-powered future - and a lot of it depends on your trust. At I/O 2026, Google described a bunch of new tools that it claims will make your life easier. Gemini...

item.title: Introducing OpenAI for Singapore
item.description: OpenAI for Singapore launches a multi-year AI partnership to expand deployment, build local talent, and support businesses and public services with AI.

item.title: Here are our favorite Memorial Day deals (so far)
item.description: Memorial Day marks the unofficial start of summer, and the holiday’s sales include plenty of deals on gadgets that can help you make the most of the season. If your plans involve beach days, pool...

item.title: Roundtables: Inside the Musk v. Altman Trial
item.description: Listen to the session or watch below Elon Musk lost his suit against OpenAI, in which he alleged CEO Sam Altman and President Greg Brockman had deceived him over the company’s non-profit status....

item.title: Democrats preview how they’d go after the Ticketmaster settlement if they regain power
item.description: A handful of Democrats called an unofficial hearing on Capitol Hill Monday to slam the Department of Justice's "trivial" and "pathetic" settlement with Live Nation-Ticketmaster, previewing how...

item.title: Elon Musk said Sam Altman ‘stole’ a non-profit — but the trial showed he had similar aims
item.description: The jury's speedy decision to reject Elon Musk's lawsuit against the other founders of OpenAI and Microsoft confirmed what we saw in the courtroom: Musk's case was a weak one, in part because he...

item.title: Everything Announced at Google I/O 2026: Gemini, Search, Smart Glasses
item.description: Google is sprucing up its Gemini models, revamping search, and enabling AI agents in everything. There are also some spiffy new smart glasses coming this fall.
